summary: Frontend-only testing of new Páginas module (compositor de secciones). Lista, EditorPagina, EditarSeccion, AddSectionDialog, WidgetPicker, BackgroundPicker, SectionPreview validated against URL https://estudio-panel.preview.emergentagent.com. All 5 seed pages render, all 5 seed sections in p-inicio render with correct kinds, controls and live sync work. One real bug: section visibility toggle does not actually visually dim the band (inline opacity overrides Tailwind class).

ui_bugs.issue: Inline style `opacity: isDragging ? 0.6 : 1` is ALWAYS applied to the <li>, overriding the Tailwind class `!section.visible && 'opacity-60'`. As a result, toggling section-toggle-s-3 (or any section switch) leaves the band visually at opacity 1 instead of 0.6 as required. Verified via getComputedStyle after clicking the switch: pre=1, post=1.

action_items: Fix Band opacity bug in EditorPagina.tsx: combine the hidden state into the inline opacity (e.g. `opacity: isDragging ? 0.6 : section.visible ? 1 : 0.6`) OR remove the inline `opacity` and rely solely on the Tailwind class. Hidden sections must visually drop to ~0.6 opacity to match spec.

Optional polish: in EditorPagina there is no list-level 'reload' persistence verification possible because Zustand is in-memory; full browser reloads reset the store (expected by design). No fix needed, just documenting.
critical_code_review_comments: Band inline-style opacity overrides class-based opacity – classic CSS specificity bug. Inline always wins.
addPage uses `Date.now()` for id which works in-memory but means refreshing page loses any new pages (acceptable for current in-memory design, called out in tests).
BG_COLOR_PRESETS labels are only present as aria-label on the swatch buttons; consider exposing them as visible tooltips/text for clarity (minor UX).

EditarSeccion uses an empty deps array for the topbar action useEffect with eslint-disable; navigate/pageId references are captured at mount which is fine here, but worth noting if pageId could change without remount.
seed_data_creation: None – relied on existing Zustand seeds (5 pages, 5 sections in p-inicio).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Verified flows: sidebar 'Páginas' entry, paginas-list shows 5 rows (p-inicio, p-artista, p-obras, p-contacto, p-galeria) with correct icons/badges (note: visibility badge text is uppercased via CSS so use case-insensitive checks for 'Visible'/'Oculta'); page-add creates a `p-<timestamp>` page and navigates to /paginas/:id; page-editor renders 5 bands s-1..s-5 in order with all controls (drag handle, icon, name, WidgetPicker, BackgroundPicker, Editar contenido, Switch, Trash); add-section-btn opens dialog with catalog-search + 6 catalog-filter-* + grid; searching 'historia' filters to 1 card; clicking catalog-card-historia adds a section (bands 5->6) and closes the dialog; WidgetPicker popover shows hero variants (Centrado, Texto + foto, Foto a pantalla completa); BackgroundPicker presets exposed via aria-labels (Crema/Blanco/Niebla/Carbón/Pizarra/Bronce/Musgo/Veneciano) plus custom color picker and hex input and 'Subir imagen de fondo'; section-editor at /paginas/p-inicio/seccion/s-1 has injected 'Volver a la página' in topbar, header 'Inicio · Cabecera (hero)', and live syncs content-title and content-cta-text to section-preview; in-memory persistence works across SPA navigation. Full browser reloads reset Zustand (by design). The only real bug found is the opacity-override on hidden bands in EditorPagina.tsx.
rca of the issue: RCA – Band opacity bug: In /app/frontend/src/pages/paginas/EditorPagina.tsx the Band component applies `style={{ ...transform, transition, opacity: isDragging ? 0.6 : 1 }}` directly on the <li>. CSS specificity: inline `style` beats class-defined opacity. Therefore the conditional Tailwind class `!section.visible && 'opacity-60'` is silently overridden. Reproduction: open /paginas/p-inicio, click [data-testid='section-toggle-s-3'], observe getComputedStyle(li).opacity remains '1'. Fix: change inline style to `opacity: isDragging ? 0.6 : section.visible ? 1 : 0.6` and optionally remove the redundant Tailwind opacity class.
